subject: economy
note: SAMPLE questions (source 'Sample (UPSC pattern)'). Replace with verified real PYQs + SME solutions + 13-language translations before shipping.

prompt.en: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), the country's central bank, was established in the year:

solution.en: The RBI was established on 1 April 1935 under the RBI Act, 1934, and was nationalised in 1949. 1969 was the year of the first bank nationalisation.

prompt.en: The body that replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015 is:

solution.en: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) replaced the Planning Commission on 1 January 2015 as the government's policy think-tank.
GDP is Gross Domestic Product — the total monetary value of all final goods and services produced within a country's borders in a given period.

prompt.en: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) came into effect in India in the year:

solution.en: GST was launched on 1 July 2017, replacing many indirect taxes with a single unified tax under the slogan 'One Nation, One Tax'.

prompt.en: Which institution has the sole authority to issue currency notes in India (except one-rupee notes)?

solution.en: The RBI is the sole issuer of banknotes in India (the one-rupee note and coins are issued by the Government of India, though circulated by the RBI).

prompt.en: The 'fiscal deficit' of a government is the excess of its:

choices.en: Total expenditure over total receipts excluding borrowings

solution.en: The fiscal deficit is the amount by which the government's total expenditure exceeds its total receipts excluding borrowings — i.e. how much it needs to borrow. The gap between exports and imports is the trade deficit.
